labels.analysis: Public MCP server "GitMCP" with 5 tools. Added from awesome-remote-mcp-servers list.
labels.reasoning: Verified as publicly accessible with no authentication required.
labels.tool_verified_reason: tools/list returned valid tools via direct MCP connection

tools.name: match_common_libs_owner_repo_mapping
tools.description: Match a library name to an owner/repo. Don't use it if you have an owner and repo already. Use this first if only a library name was provided. If found - you can use owner and repo to call other tools. If not found - try to use the library name directly in other tools.

library.description: The name of the library to try and match to an owner/repo.

tools.name: fetch_generic_documentation
tools.description: Fetch documentation for any GitHub repository by providing owner and project name

owner.description: The GitHub repository owner (username or organization)

tools.name: search_generic_documentation
tools.description: Semantically search in documentation for any GitHub repository by providing owner, project name, and search query. Useful for specific queries.

owner.description: The GitHub repository owner (username or organization)

query.description: The search query to find relevant documentation

tools.name: search_generic_code
tools.description: Search for code in any GitHub repository by providing owner, project name, and search query. Returns matching files. Supports pagination with 30 results per page.

owner.description: The GitHub repository owner (username or organization)

query.description: The search query to find relevant code files

page.description: Page number to retrieve (starting from 1). Each page contains 30 results.

tools.name: fetch_generic_url_content
tools.description: Generic tool to fetch content from any absolute URL, respecting robots.txt rules. Use this to retrieve referenced urls (absolute urls) that were mentioned in previously fetched documentation.

url.description: The URL of the document or page to fetch
